channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Tribunal rules in favour of landlord in first test of new pet rental rules
item.description: A landlord's decision to deny a tenant's request to keep a 6-month-old rescue kitten in a unit has been upheld, despite new Tasmanian laws intended to make it easier to keep pets in rentals.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: British Shorthair kitten generic.

item.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians
item.description: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.

media:group.media:description: Home care price caps to stop older Australians being overcharged have been deferred indefinitely.

item.title: Reserve Bank worries about inflation pressures building, risk of a recession
item.description: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Sarah Hunter has spoken at an investment gathering in Sydney.

item.title: 'Policy decisions' the cause of ACT's fiscal deterioration, economist says
item.description: An independent economist warns Canberrans will need to pay higher taxes and charges if the ACT government continues the same amount of spending on infrastructure and services.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: Economist Saul Eslake says the ACT government spent more on delivering services and providing new infrastructure without raising sufficient additional revenue.

item.title: Church pulls out all the stops for its pipe organ restoration
item.description: A restoration project which saw a church organ deconstructed pipe-by-pipe is now ready to play again.
item.dc:creator: Kemii Maguire

media:group.media:description: John Standley plays an organ pipe, removed from its resting place in the church.

item.title: Death penalty rates at 40-year high globally, Amnesty International says
item.description: A crackdown in Iran drove a surge in the number of people executed worldwide in 2025, but China remains the "leading executioner," human rights group Amnesty International says.
item.dc:creator: Doug Dingwall

media:group.media:description: Activists attend a candlelight vigil in February 2025 against the execution of a man in Singapore for drug trafficking.

item.title: Breaking: Ex-GP charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault
item.description: A former Brisbane doctor has been charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault following an extensive police investigation.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: Dr Stan Theodoros is facing charges of rape and sexual assault.

item.title: Why Cuba is warning of a 'bloodbath' if Trump launches military action
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez says Cuba's right to defend itself should not be used by the US as justification for a potential war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Cooper

media:group.media:description: Miguel Diaz-Canel has described recent US sanctions against his nation as a "genocidal siege".

item.title: Masked band TISM stung with $18,000 Sydney Opera House fine
item.description: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with an hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: A photo of TISM promoting their Sydney Opera House performance, April 2026.

item.title: Why Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I
item.description: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Sam Walker will make his Queensland debut in State of Origin I next Wednesday.

item.title: NT records two deaths from mosquito-borne disease Murray Valley encephalitis
item.description: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

media:group.media:description: Murray Valley encephalitis is transmitted by mosquitoes carrying the virus.

item.title: Mayor defends plan to introduce parking meters at popular Sydney beaches
item.description: A recent survey of residents found 54 per cent supported the plan that would introduce paid parking for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Parking meters will be introduced at several Sydney beaches but locals would be exempt through the use of permits.

item.title: Eligibility expanded for Queensland's needle-free flu vaccine program
item.description: With stocks set to expire in August, the FluMist nasal spray has been made free for all children over the age of two.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Clarke

media:group.media:description: Queensland's FluMist vaccine program has been expanded to include children aged between six and 17.

item.title: Organic meat giant Hewitt to buy Nolan Meats for undisclosed sum
item.description: Australia's largest organic meat company has agreed to buy the family-owned meatworks, subject to conditions and regulatory approvals.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Nichols and Kallee Buchanan

media:group.media:description: The Hewitts grew up in central Queensland with big dreams.

item.title: Pheobe Bishop murder-accused's defence waiting for ballistics report
item.description: The defence team for Tanika Bromley, who is charged over the murder of Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop, raises issues about delays in receiving parts of the brief of evidence.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Tanika Bromley remains in custody.

item.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle
item.description: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Morris-Grant

media:group.media:description: The lawsuit between OpenAI's Sam Altman and one of its original co-founders, Elon Musk, has reached its end.

item.title: 'Better for victims': $18.9 million invested in director of public prosecutions
item.description: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.
item.dc:creator: Niki Burnside

media:group.media:description: A funding boost for the office of the director of public prosecutions has been announced by the ACT government following lobbying from the chief prosecutor.

item.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque
item.description: Two teenagers opened fire at the largest mosque in San Diego, California on Monday, killing three men. Here is what we know about the attack.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Police vehicles crowded nearby streets after the "active shooter situation" was reported.

item.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses
item.description: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.
item.dc:creator: Sam Bradbrook, Sally Bryant, and Becc Bird

media:group.media:description: Viewers in affected regions would lose access to Network 10, as well as its secondary channels, as part of the deal lapsing.

item.title: Farm worker given $5,000 cash to perform fatal amputation, court hears
item.description: Kalman Tal, 66, died soon after he asked for his left foot to be cut off in a public park with a battery-powered saw, a court has heard, but the man who performed the amputation has pleaded not guilty to murder.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: John Yalu is standing trial for murder at the Supreme Court in Cairns.

item.title: Woman charged over death of 79yo man after alleged 'road rage' attack
item.description: A woman has faced court over the death of a man she allegedly attacked in a "road rage" incident at a shopping centre car park on the NSW South Coast.
item.dc:creator: Charles Rushforth

media:group.media:description: Eliza Wellington, who has her face covered, is guided into Nowra court.

item.title: Power network's $10 million plan to combat SA's repeated regional blackouts
item.description: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.
item.dc:creator: Arj Ganesan

media:group.media:description: When the sun sets, electricity costs rise, as many consumers are finding out directly.

item.title: Magpies struck by deadly mystery illness as carers report declining numbers
item.description: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Williams, Pip Waller, and Brianna Melville

media:group.media:description: The magpies affected by the disease often cannot move their legs or are very weak.

item.title: Apology for 'appalling' display of autopsy body parts without consent
item.description: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.
item.dc:creator: Adam Holmes

media:group.media:description: Some of the body parts displayed in the University of Tasmania's RA Rodda Pathology Museum were obtained without consent.

item.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child
item.description: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

media:group.media:description: KA Rung had Filipino cultural markings done last year.
